rule: # Salesforce Code Analyzer Waiver Register
# Managed by: DevOps / Tech Lead  |  Branch: main  |  Path: .github/sf-scanner-waivers.csv
# SCHEMA
#   rule              REQUIRED  Rule name as reported in SF Scanner CSV output (substring match)
#   file_pattern      REQUIRED  Filename as reported in SF Scanner CSV output (substring match
#   message_contains  OPTIONAL  Substring of the violation message to narrow the match (leave blank = match all messages)
#   severity_threshold OPTIONAL  Only waive violations at this severity or above (leave blank = match any severity)
#   expiry            REQUIRED  Hard deadline YYYY-MM-DD - pipeline FAILS after this date if violation persists
#   reason            REQUIRED  Business justification. Include Jira/ticket reference.
#   approved_by       REQUIRED  GitHub username of approver (Tech Lead / DevOps / Security team)
#   approved_date     REQUIRED  Date approval was granted YYYY-MM-DD
#   ticket            REQUIRED  Jira or GitHub issue tracking the fix (e.g. PROJ-123)
#   status            REQUIRED  ACTIVE = waiver in force | REVOKED = waiver cancelled (row kept for audit)
# GOVERNANCE
#   1. Developer identifies a violation that cannot be fixed immediately
#   2. Developer raises a PR against main adding a waiver row here (fully populated)
#   3. Tech Lead reviews justification
#   4. Tech Lead approves and merges - approved_by must match their GitHub username
#   5. Developer sets a reminder before expiry date to fix the violation or request renewal
#   6. Once fixed
file_pattern: update status to REVOKED (do NOT delete - preserves audit trail)

rule: #   7. Critical violations (severity 1–2) require Security/DevOps approval; max 7-day waiver
